description: Letter from Yeshu'a ha-Kohen ha-Ḥaver, an official in Alexandria, to Nahray b. Nissim. Yeshu'a writes to raise money to free three Jews held by Italian merchants. The merchants had acquired their human cargo from Rum (Byzantine) pirates. The pirates had beaten and almost killed their captives. Jewish communities in Egyptian port towns bore the brunt of these expenses. Charitable collections were often held throughout the Jewish communities of Egypt to help free co-religionists captured in wartime or in acts of piracy. (Information from CUDL)
tags: Byzantines, trade, Byzantium, Italy, captives, pirates, Yeshu'a ha-Kohen, Nahray B. Nissim, merchants
description_he: מכתב מישועה הכהן החבר, אלכסנדריה, לנהריי בן נסים. על גיוס כסף לפדיון שלושה יהודים שנמצאים בידי סוחרים איטלקים שרכשו אותם מפיראטים ביזנטיים.
